summary: Clean implementation of automatic optical sizing. Two well-documented public functions (compute_optical_size, auto_axis_values_for_opsz) with correct typographic formula (px × 72 / dpi). Proper clamping to font's declared opsz range. Graceful no-op for non-opsz fonts. Pipeline wiring through GlyphCache::populate_with_dpi() is minimal and correct. SCREEN_DPI constant in compositor is appropriately documented as hardcoded for QEMU. 8 tests cover all validation assertions.

observations: Functions are pure and well-documented with doc comments explaining the typographic formula
Edge case handling is correct: dpi=0 fallback, empty font data returns empty Vec
The populate_with_dpi method correctly delegates to populate for backward compatibility
SCREEN_DPI is hardcoded but documented as configurable in principle — appropriate for prototype stage
The formula opsz = font_size_px × 72 / dpi correctly converts pixel size to point size
Clamping to font's opsz axis range prevents invalid axis values
rasterize_with_axes correctly delegates to standard rasterize() when axes are empty
Auto-opsz axes are computed once per populate call, not per-glyph — efficient
VAL-OPSZ-001: 3 size/DPI combos produce distinct values — verified
VAL-OPSZ-002: 10px vs 48px produce different coverage with real font — verified
VAL-OPSZ-003: No-op for non-opsz fonts with byte-identical output — verified
Additional edge cases: DPI=72 identity, empty font data, static fonts
No user input, no IPC changes, no security-sensitive code
